textPlain: > I was recently working on a project that uses the AWS SDK for JavaScript. When updating the dependencies in said project, I noticed that the version of that dependency was v3.888.0. Eight hundred eighty eight. That’s a big number as far as versions go.It also isn’t the first AWS SDK. A few of us in… 2012 IIRC… wrote the first one because AWS didn’t think node was worth an SDK.
I wonder if the author could have replicated the couchdb database locally to make their life easier.
